name: Ngai
description: Ngai is the supreme God in Kikuyu mythology, the creator and sustainer of all life. He is a monotheistic deity, revered as the all-powerful and benevolent force behind the universe. Ngai resides on Mount Kenya (Kirinyaga), considered sacred by the Kikuyu people. He is the giver of rain, sunshine, fertility, and all good things. While Ngai is generally seen as benevolent, he can also inflict punishment for wrongdoing. He is not depicted in physical form, but is understood to be a spiritual presence that permeates all of creation. Sacrifices and prayers are offered to Ngai to seek his blessings and guidance. The Kikuyu traditionally believed that Ngai chose them to inhabit the land and entrusted them with its care. He is the ultimate authority and the source of all morality and justice. The worship of Ngai is central to Kikuyu identity and worldview, emphasizing a deep connection to the land and a respect for the natural world. Stories of Ngai are passed down through generations, reinforcing the community's values and beliefs.
subtitle: The Supreme Creator God of the Kikuyu

keyMyths.title: Ngai and the First Man
keyMyths.description: Ngai created the first man, Gikuyu, and placed him near Mount Kenya. He then gave Gikuyu a wife, Mumbi, and bestowed upon them the land and its resources.

keyMyths.title: The Offering of Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: When the Kikuyu people face hardship, they offer sacrifices to Ngai at the sacred Mugumo tree. These offerings are meant to appease Ngai and seek his intervention.

keyMyths.title: Ngai's Wrath
keyMyths.description: If the Kikuyu people disobey Ngai's laws or disrespect the land, Ngai may send drought or other calamities as punishment. This serves as a reminder of his power and the importance of righteousness.

sources.author: Jomo Kenyatta
